title: 11 Herbal Teas For Acid Reflux
intro_desc: Herbal teas can be a great relief for people who suffer from acid reflux. This is because many herbs have natural properties that help calm down the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. For instance, Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe the stomach lining and reduce acid production. Drinking ginger tea can bring quick relief from heartburn and discomfort.  Another herb that's commonly used to treat acid reflux is Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root. The active compound in licorice root, called glycyrrhizin, has anti-inflammatory properties that help heal the stomach lining and reduce acid production. It also helps relax the muscles in the digestive tract, which can help prevent acid from flowing back up into the esophagus.  Fennel, or Foeniculum vulgare, is another herb that can help alleviate acid reflux symptoms. The essential oils in fennel seeds have natural antispasmodic properties that help calm down the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. Drinking fennel tea can help relieve heartburn, bloating, and discomfort associated with acid reflux.  Drinking herbal teas can bring several benefits to your life. Not only can they help alleviate acid reflux symptoms, but they can also promote overall digestive health. They can also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate acid reflux symptoms. Additionally, many herbal teas are caffeine-free, making them a great alternative to traditional tea or coffee. By incorporating herbal teas into your daily routine, you can find natural relief from acid reflux and enjoy a healthier, happier digestive system.
faq_1: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent acid reflux from forming. Ginger tea, in particular, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can help prevent acid reflux. Other herbal teas like peppermint and chamomile may also help calm the stomach and reduce symptoms of acid reflux.
faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea might help ease acid reflux symptoms, but it's not a guarantee it'll prevent them from forming. Ginger tea, in particular, has anti-inflammatory properties that could help calm the digestive system. Other herbal teas like chamomile and peppermint might also provide some relief, but their effectiveness can vary from person to person.
faq_safe: While herbal teas can be a soothing choice for acid reflux, it's essential to be mindful of how often you consume them. Drinking them daily can sometimes trigger more acid production, exacerbating the issue. It's best to try them in moderation, perhaps 2-3 times a week, to see if they help alleviate symptoms without causing any discomfort.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with acid reflux symptoms. Some people notice relief within a few days to a week of drinking them regularly. Others might feel better after a couple of weeks. Everyone's body is different, so it's hard to pinpoint an exact time frame for results.
faq_time: For acid reflux, it's a good idea to drink herbal tea when you're about to eat or after a meal. This can help calm your stomach and reduce symptoms. Try having a cup about 30 minutes before eating or within an hour after eating to see if it helps.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for acid reflux. For example, peppermint tea can slow down digestion and worsen symptoms. Ginger tea may interact with antacids, reducing their effectiveness. Some herbal teas, like licorice root, contain glycyrrhizin, which can increase stomach acid, making symptoms worse.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's a good idea to be cautious with herbal teas for acid reflux. Some herbs, like ginger, may be okay in small amounts, but others, like peppermint and chamomile, can relax the muscles too much and worsen symptoms. It's best to stick to small amounts and avoid any that might affect your baby's growth or health.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, it's generally a good idea to be careful with herbal teas for acid reflux. Some herbs, like peppermint and ginger, might help soothe your symptoms, but others, like licorice root, could be too strong for your body. If you're concerned, try a small amount first to see how you react.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas contains active constituents like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, a common cause of acid reflux. The gingerols and shogaols also have a relaxing effect on the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing food to move more smoothly and reducing pressure on the stomach. Additionally, the warming properties of ginger help increase blood flow to the digestive tract, improving digestion and reducing symptoms of acid reflux. By reducing inflammation and improving digestion, Zingiber officinale teas may help alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1/2 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root.
Heat the water in a pot until it boils.
Reduce heat and add the dried Zingiber officinale root to the water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes to release the flavors.
Strain the tea and drink while warm, 2-3 times a day as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas contains active compounds like glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and triterpenoids. These constituents have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and ease acid reflux symptoms. Glycyrrhizin, in particular, has been shown to decrease the production of stomach acid, making it a potential natural remedy for acid reflux. The flavonoids present in the tea also have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the lining of the esophagus from damage caused by stomach acid. By reducing inflammation and decreasing stomach acid production, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas may provide relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Get 1 tablespoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the 1 tablespoon of dried roots to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it when it's warm or cool.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare teas contains compounds like anethole and limonene, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, a common cause of acid reflux. Anethole also has a relaxing effect on the muscles in the digestive tract, which can help prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. Limonene has a soothing effect on the stomach lining, reducing irritation and inflammation that can contribute to acid reflux. By reducing inflammation and soothing the stomach lining, Foeniculum vulgare teas can help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal teas for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Get 1 teaspoon of dried Foeniculum vulgare seeds.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the dried Foeniculum vulgare seeds to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 to 1 cup before meals to help with acid reflux.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita teas contains compounds like menthol, menthone, and limonene, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, a common cause of acid reflux. The menthol in Mentha x piperita teas also helps relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms that can cause stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus. Additionally, the essential oils in Mentha x piperita teas have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that can trigger acid reflux. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the digestive muscles, Mentha x piperita teas can provide relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal teas for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Mentha x piperita leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 tablespoon of honey or sugar (optional) and set aside.
Steep the Mentha x piperita leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Add the measured honey or sugar (if using) and stir to dissolve before drinking.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas contains quinine and other alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Quinine has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, a common cause of acid reflux. The alkaloid cinchonine has a relaxing effect on the muscles in the stomach and esophagus, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. The tea's bitter compounds, such as quinovic acid, stimulate digestion and improve the movement of food through the digestive system, reducing the symptoms of acid reflux. By reducing inflammation and improving digestion, Cinchona officinalis teas can provide relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal teas for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Cinchona officinalis bark, a cup of boiling water, and a heat-resistant cup.
Add the dried Cinchona officinalis bark to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the bark.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey (optional) to the tea and stir well.
Drink the tea slowly and see the effect. Start with a small amount and adjust according to your body's response.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zanthoxylum bungeanum teas contains bioactive constituents such as ferulic acid, cinnamic acid, and isoquercitrin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, a common cause of acid reflux. The ferulic acid in Zanthoxylum bungeanum teas has also been shown to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. Additionally, the cinnamic acid in the tea has antioxidant properties that help neutralize free radicals that can damage the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, further exacerbating acid reflux. By reducing inflammation and protecting the mucous membranes, Zanthoxylum bungeanum teas may provide relief for individuals suffering from acid reflux.
remedies.image_alt: Zanthoxylum bungeanum herbal teas for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Zanthoxylum bungeanum leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the leaves and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the leaves. Add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate acid reflux symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum teas contains a compound called cinnamaldehyde, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. This compound helps to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. Cinnamomum verum teas also contains cineole, a natural antispasmodic that reduces inflammation and eases digestive discomfort. The warm and soothing properties of cinnamomum verum teas can help to calm the digestive system and reduce symptoms of acid reflux, such as heartburn and bloating. Regular consumption of cinnamomum verum teas may help to reduce the frequency and severity of acid reflux episodes.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal teas for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of Cinnamomum verum powder, 1 cup of water, and a strainer.
Boil the water in a pot and let it cool for 1 minute.
Add the Cinnamomum verum powder to the water and stir well.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into a cup.
Drink the tea slowly and avoid swallowing air to help with acid reflux.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum teas contains silymarin, a mixture of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, polyphenols, and terpenoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. The flavonoids and polyphenols in silymarin can also protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus from acid damage, preventing acid reflux. The terpenoids in silymarin have a relaxing effect on the muscles in the digestive tract, which can help reduce acid production and improve digestion. By reducing inflammation and improving digestion, silymarin in Silybum marianum teas can help alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal teas for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Silybum marianum flowers. You can buy them online or at a local store.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried flowers and put them in a tea infuser.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot. Let it cool for 1 minute.
Pour the hot water over the tea infuser with the Silybum marianum flowers. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it. You can add honey or lemon to taste.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas contains linalool and linalyl acetate, which are its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. Lavandula angustifolia tea's calming effects can also help to relax the muscles in the stomach and lower the production of stomach acid, alleviating symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, the tea's ability to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation can help to decrease the frequency of acid reflux episodes. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Lavandula angustifolia tea may provide relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers or 2 tablespoons of dried flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a kettle or pot.
Steep the flowers in the hot water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Drink 1/2 cup of the Lavandula angustifolia tea, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe acid reflux.
Add a tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, for extra soothing benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are the bioactive constituents responsible for its therapeutic properties. Flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, a common cause of acid reflux. Terpenoids, particularly ginkgolides, have antioxidant properties that protect the mucous membranes from oxidative stress, further reducing acid reflux symptoms. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Ginkgo biloba help soothe and protect the digestive tract, reducing the frequency and severity of acid reflux episodes. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Ginkgo biloba teas can provide relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal teas for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and stir well.
Drink the Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day, after meals to help manage acid reflux.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa teas contains a compound called curcumin, which is the primary bioactive constituent responsible for its therapeutic properties. Curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the esophagus and stomach, common causes of acid reflux. The antioxidant properties of curcumin also help to protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, curcumin has a relaxing effect on the lower esophageal sphincter, which helps to prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. The combination of these properties makes Curcuma longa teas a potential natural remedy for alleviating acid reflux symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal teas for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Curcuma longa powder and 1 cup of water.
Heat the water in a saucepan over low heat.
Add the Curcuma longa powder to the water and stir well.
Let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into a cup.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, 2-3 times a day as needed.
